title: Grafana SSO / RBAC access configuration
description: One record per Grafana instance/org summarizing its authentication / authorization CONFIGURATION: whether SSO is enabled, the per-provider SSO posture (provider type + enabled state + org-role mapping RULE names), aggregate team counts (team count + total team-membership COUNT), and the RBAC role-assignment COUNTS rolled up by scope (user / team / built-in). Pulled read-only from the Grafana API (GET /api/v1/sso-settings, GET /api/teams/search, GET /api/access-control/assignments). This is the slice-534 sibling of monitoring.alert_config.v1 (slice 488 deferred the authn/authz surface, P0-488-7): SSO enforcement + RBAC posture is an IAM surface, not a monitoring surface, so it gets its own kind (IAC-06 Authenticator Management + IAC-22 Least Privilege; SOC 2 CC6.1/CC6.2/CC6.3). CONFIGURATION + COUNTS only — NEVER a SAML private key, an OAuth client secret, an LDAP bind password, a signing certificate, a user name/email/login, or any individual team-member or role-assignment principal identity. The connector reports a descriptive enabled/posture/count summary; the platform evaluator owns the final pass/fail per (control, scope).

sso_enabled.description: True if ANY SSO provider is enabled — the CC6.1 'is SSO enforced' headline. A descriptive flag, never a credential.

team_count.description: Number of teams configured. A COUNT, never the team member identities.

total_team_memberships.description: Sum of team memberships across all teams. A COUNT, never the member identities (no name / email / login is collected).

user_role_assignments.description: Count of RBAC role assignments scoped to individual users. A COUNT, never the assigned principals.

team_role_assignments.description: Count of RBAC role assignments scoped to teams. A COUNT, never the assigned principals.

builtin_role_assignments.description: Count of RBAC role assignments scoped to built-in roles. A COUNT, never the assigned principals.

sso_providers.description: Per-provider SSO configuration — provider TYPE + enabled state + org-role mapping RULE names only. NEVER the SAML private key, OAuth client secret, LDAP bind password, signing certificate, or any identity.

provider_type.description: The SSO provider type (e.g. 'saml', 'oauth', 'oidc', 'ldap', 'github', 'google'). The TYPE, never a secret.

enabled.description: Whether this SSO provider is currently enabled.

role_mappings.description: The org-role mapping RULE strings the provider applies (role names / attribute->role expressions, e.g. 'Editor', 'Admin', 'GrafanaAdmin'). Role NAMES / rule strings only — never an identity, never a secret.
